0.response: {"Q0":"I tried to quickly estimate which ratio is more profitable for the galaxies. For the planets, once the red gems were no longer profitable, I thought of the red planet as undesirable as the yellow planet, which allowed me to quickly associate it as an unwanted thing. I think that helped me a lot. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"Just estimating which ratio or percentage of odds, coupled with the amount of dollars, was more beneficially proportionate toward a higher reward. "}

9.response: {"Q0":"Everything was clear and thorough. Thank you for the clear instructions and this fun gamified study! It was a lot of fun. "}

1.response: {"Q0":"The strategy I didn't mention before was that I used saying \"upper\" or \"lower\" out loud to help drive my focus on the upper or lower galaxy, whichever appeared more profitable, and I kept it steady throughout the whole game. It helped me keep track of where to look and to keep my focus steady. "}

5.response: {"Q0":"I think I only did once or twice, once perhaps partially. It almost felt like an impulse, as there's a reward I was already used to. But once I clearly associated it as undesirable, like the yellow planet, it made its \"relevance\" fade away. "}

2.response: {"Q0":"I would say gems would pop up somewhere between 3 and 4 times. Maybe about once every 3.5 to 3.8 turns. But if I had to pick a single number overall, then about 1 out of 4 times. "}

6.response: {"Q0":"I think that's where I accidentally clicked one red one, by accident, but it was not more than one. It might've been a misclick or a subconscious action. Can't tell you for sure. "}

8.response: {"Q0":"Yes, I was having a difficult time figuring out quick on which galaxy to pick, so then I began repeating out loud, once or twice, which future galaxy to pick so that I wouldn't forget which one appeared preferrable, as the other task would often make me lose focus. But once I got the hang of it, it was smooth sailing from that point on. "}

3.response: {"Q0":"Absolutely. I almost instantly associated the red planet as undesirable, and I did that by associating it with the yellow planet. That was the easiest way for me to think of it as unwanted, and it helped quite a bit. At the same time, I just focused on only focusing on the blue planet, since it was the only important planet from that point on. In the first few rounds, it was still tough to \"let go\" of the red planet, but after a minute or two, it became natural to associate as non-relevant, just like the yellow planet. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes, the astronaut leaving meant that the germs were no longer valuable and wouldn't up my gold count. It was very clear and the cute little astronaut explained it thoroughly with plenty of time given to fully process it. "}

10.response: {"Q0":"I didn't experience any bugs or technical problems, besides the screen going dark once or twice for a few seconds, but then moving my mouse made it appear back again. "}

11.response: {"Q0":"It was a lovely study that challenged my focus and rapid thinking or decision-making process. I appreciate it very much and look forward to doing more, if they will be around. Thank you! "}
